evidence.q1: Sections numbered 1–15 sequentially; Section 8 cross-reference 'Notwithstanding Section 3' resolves correctly to existing Section 3.
evidence.q2: Section 2 defines 'Digital Assets', 'Customer Assets', 'Authorized Instruction'; each term used consistently thereafter (e.g., 'Customer Assets' in Section 1, 3, 4, etc., 'Authorized Instruction' in Section 1, 8).
evidence.q3: No vague time/quantity phrasing; all are specific: 'within 24 hours', '2 business days', '5 business days', '10 business days', '10 days', 'prompt notice', 'annual' SOC report.
evidence.q4: No archaic or out-of-register diction; entire document uses plain modern English (e.g., 'Custodian will provide', 'Customer retains', 'commercially reasonable', not 'heretofore' or 'witnesseth').
evidence.q5: No internally contradictory or grammatically broken sentence; e.g., Section 13 liability cap and carve-out read coherently, and Section 8 liquidity deployment is logically structured.
evidence.q6: Register coherent across all sections; consistent neutral/modern commercial phrasing (e.g., 'Custodian will', 'Customer may', no section reads as if by a different author).
